notes: umb_review achieved 100% pass rate across all 15 assertions — perfect score on format, content, and correctness
code_review consistently failed format assertions (uses its own '### Code review' format, no target branch or verdict) — expected since it's a different tool with different output format
no_skill performed well on content assertions (73%) but always failed the structured format check — Claude Opus 4.6 is capable without the skill but inconsistent on format
umb_review is slower (381s avg vs 167s code_review, 283s no_skill) — the structured multi-step workflow adds overhead
The 'structured-format' assertion passes only for umb_review by design — it tests the skill's specific output format. This accounts for 3 of code_review's 7 failures and 3 of no_skill's 4 failures
All 3 configs correctly identified the alias rename breaking change in PR#22214 — this is a real issue, not a false positive
code_review struggled most on PR#22217 (25% pass rate) — its confidence filtering approach missed behavioral impact analysis entirely
